name: MCA
mission: To provide religious, social, and educational services to enable the Muslim community to fulfill its responsibilities and contribute to the betterment o...
zakatClaimEvidence: The Muslim Community Association (MCA) explicitly states, "Submit your Zakah request to the Muslim Community Association." and "MCA's core social service is the collection of Zakat and Sadaqah funds to support people in need...". They also have a dedicated "Zakah Request" section on their website under services and mention that "Zakat Al-Fitr will be collected starting March 9th." Furthermore, they have a "Zakah and Relief committee" chartered to facilitate the distribution of Zakah monies. (Source: https://www.mcabayarea.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Islamic identity (+1); Asnaf: masakin (+5); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.

components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish current reserve levels and a board-approved reserve policy (often 3-12 months for most nonprofits).

score_details.score_summary: Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area shows good alignment and limited impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: The Muslim Community Association (MCA) explicitly states, "Submit your Zakah request to the Muslim Community Association." and "MCA's core social service is the collection of Zakat and Sadaqah funds to support people in need...". They also have a dedicated "Zakah Request" section on their website under services and mention that "Zakat Al-Fitr will be collected starting March 9th." Furthermore, they have a "Zakah and Relief committee" chartered to facilitate the distribution of Zakah monies. (Source: https://www.mcabayarea.org)

all_citations.claim: The organization has profile metadata available but lacks a formal Charity Navigator rating score.
Financial data and compensation records are filed under EIN 31-1267559.
The organization operates a Weekend Islamic School and provides social services and Zakah distribution.
all_citations.source_name: Donate stock to mca – download file

all_citations.claim: MCA provides religious, social, and educational services to enable the Muslim community to fulfill its responsibilities.
The organization is Zakat-eligible and offers various donation methods for community support.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a moderate score of 45/100, reflecting a strong cultural alignment and direct service model balanced against significant gaps in financial data transparency.
areas_for_improvement: Limited public financial transparency regarding program expense ratios
Lack of third-party impact verification or formal ratings

dimension_explanations.alignment: With a score of 32/50, the organization shows high alignment for Muslim donors due to its Zakat-eligible status and focus on urgent religious and cultural needs [5]. It maintains a long-standing track record as a central institution for the San Francisco Bay Area community, though data confidence remains medium due to limited external reporting [1][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The impact score of 18/50 reflects a direct service model that provides essential religious and educational resources, though effectiveness is difficult to quantify due to a lack of publicly available cost-per-beneficiary data [1][2]. While the organization facilitates critical services like Zakah distribution, the absence of detailed financial metrics limits the ability to assess long-term program efficiency [3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area serves as a vital hub for religious, social, and educational empowerment within the regional Muslim community.
strengths: Designated as Zakat-eligible for religious giving
Offers diverse programming including educational and social services
Provides multiple giving options including stock donations
baseline_narrative.summary: The Muslim Community Association of San Francisco Bay Area (MCA) provides a comprehensive suite of services including social assistance, Zakah distribution, and a dedicated Weekend Islamic School [3][4]. As a Zakat-eligible organization, it enables donors to fulfill religious obligations while contributing to the betterment of society through structured community programs [5].
